Office of the Secretary of State
March Fong Err
Executive Office
1230 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814
(916) 44s-637r
May 30, 1990
Kathryn E
Counsel
Donovan, General
Fair PoIiticaI Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804-0807
Re:
Request for Informal Assistance
California BaIlot Pamphlet
Dear Kathryn:
Secretary of State Eu is sponsoring legislation authored
by Assemblyman Chacon which would move the printing of the
text of proposed measures to the back of the California
BaIlot Pamphlet rather than beginning the prinLing on the
second page in many cases as provided for by Government
Code section 88002(c).
we believe that consolidating the text in the back of the
pamphtet wiII make the text and the other information
easier to read by not breaking up the flow. 
We believe it
will also make the titles 
and summaries, the analyses, and
the arguments and rebuttals less "intimidating" since
there will- be less printed rnaterial in these areas.
Moving the text to the back of the pamphlet will also
provide more "white space" in the title 
and summary and
analysis areas of the pamphlet which we propose to utilize
by increasing the print size for these portions of the
pamphlet.
We would very much like to use this revised format for the
November 1990 General Election pamphlet and the proposed
Iegislation wilI contain an "urgency" clause to provide
for such. However, given the scheduled legislative recess
in JuIy and the twelve day public review period for
proposed amendments set forth in Government Code section
c,
F.
rr!
c.cr
€
-fi
@


81012, we are afraid that there may not be time for
legislation to be chapterecl by our .luly 24 cleadline for
putting the final text of the pamphlet on display. 
Our
question for you, therefore, is as follows: 
Can the
Secretary of State, administratively, consolidate the
printing of the complete text of proposed measures in the
back of the California Ballot Pamphlet or is she bound by
the rigid format set forth in Government Code section
880 02?
Your early advice in this
appreciated.
regard would be very much
{.t-

from: Secretarv of State March FonE Eu
' 1230 J Street, Sacramento, CA"95814
For Immediate Release
Contact: Melissa Warren or
Shirley l{ashington
June 11, 1990
EU TO INCLUDE CANDIDATE STATEMENTS IN NOVEMBER BALLOT PAMPHLET
SACRAMENTO - Ca-Iifornia Seeretary of State March Fong Eu today (Jun. 11)
announced plans to include gubernatorial candidate statements in the Nov. gen-
eral election ballot pamphlet and eneouraged eaeh of the candidates for governor
to submit a statement.
ItIn the interest of providing voters with as much information as possible, I
have asked eaeh of the nominees for governor to submit a 200 word statement of
his or her qualifications and/or platform to be included in the state bBllot
pamphlet distributed to all voter households," Dr. Eu explained. i'Anyone who
intends to qualify as an independent eandidate for that office is also encour-
aged to submit a statement.rl
Statements may be no more than 200 words in length, may include an address or
telephone number where voters can obtain additional information and may make no
referenee to other eandidates or to another candidate,s qualifieations, charae-
ter or aetivites. They must be submitted to Cathy Mitehell, seeretary of
statets eleetions division, 1230 J St., Rm 232, Sacramento 95814 not later than
Jul. 20 if they are to appear in the pamphlet. They will not be made available
for publie review until after that deadline.
(916) +45-6375
' ta:
(over)


EU_P.2
Political party statements of philosophy and purpose were included for the
first time in the Nov. 1988 pamphlet and are expeeted to be included again this
year.
It ,, ,t

March Fong Eu

1230 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

o

c3
June 14, 1990
Scott Hallabrin, Act i ng
Generai Counsel
California Fair PoIitical Practices
Commi s s ion
428 ,-l Street, Suite 800
Sacrarnento, Ca li fornia 95804-0807
Re: California Ba 11o t Pamphlet
Dear Mr
HaIlabrin:
Believe it or not, this is NoT a request for informal
advice with respect to the publication and distribution of
the California Ballot Pamphlet. Surprise! However, I did
want to advise the Commission through your office of
several plans with respect to the November 1990 General
Election pamphlet.
First. as we did in 1988. we plan to include statements
from the five qualified political parties as to their
policies, platforms anal programs. Second, for the first
time, we plan to inclucle statements of the five nominees
(plus any inclependent nominees) for Governor. See
enclosure. Third, we are considering including a summary
page indicating in abbreviated format what "Yes" and "No"
votes mean with respect to each measure. Some
jurisdictions have done this successfully with respect to
locaf measures as have some other states. Fourth, we are
exploring the possibility of including a questionnaire to
voters. This questionnaire, to be developed irl
conjunction with the Department of Political Science at
the University of California, Davis, would survey voLers
to determine who reads the pamphlet. which parts are the
most helpful, and how the pamphlet can be made more useful
to voters. with respect to these four items, we believe
that we have the authority to include such things pursuant
to Government Code section 88001(e).
,,:
1"


2
In addition to enlarging the printed pamphlet, we plan on
proclucing, in conjunction with the California voter
Foundation, audio cassette tapes of the analyses and pro
and con arguments and rebuttals. 
Such tapes would not, of
course, fa11 within the purview of the Act.
If you should have any questions or concerns regarding are
plans with respect to the 1990 General Election California
Batlot Pamphlet, please let me know as soon as possible.
Enc lo su re
ffiMII,LER
{ cnier oeputr
